title: Planet Labs Stock Surges 22.1% on European Defense Contracts and Military Satellite Launch
text: Planet Labs PBC's stock jumped 22.1% following significant developments in its government contracts portfolio and satellite operations. The company successfully deployed three AI-enabled Pelican satellites this month, including Sweden's first sovereign military satellite under a satellite services agreement. The new spacecraft have achieved initial orbital contact and are entering the commissioning phase. Planet's European subsidiaries secured fresh, ESA-backed, seven-figure, multi-year contracts with Greece and the Czech Republic. These agreements underscore expanding governmental demand for the company's high-resolution Earth observation and analytical capabilities. The ESA-backed imagery contract with Greece directly reinforces the satellite services and solutions narrative that underpins expectations for stronger, more predictable revenue. This development demonstrates Planet's ability to diversify its revenue base beyond large defense and intelligence contracts, potentially reducing customer concentration risks. Investors should remain cautious about the company's dependency on a relatively limited pool of major government contracts, which could introduce volatility into future financial performance.
